definition: Billing provider NPI.
missing_value_logic: Not missing.

definition: Cohort identifier for the provider (e.g. NY_individual, CA_organization).
missing_value_logic: None if cohort_csv not provided.
notes: Allows filtering/grouping by cohort in downstream analysis.

definition: Calendar month (first day of month).
missing_value_logic: Not missing.

definition: Total paid amount for provider in month.
formula_or_logic: Sum of paid_amount over all rows for (npi, month).
missing_value_logic: Not missing when row exists.

definition: Total claims count for provider in month.
missing_value_logic: Same as paid_t.

definition: Number of distinct HCPCS codes used by provider in month.
missing_value_logic: 0 if inactive.

definition: Average paid per claim.
missing_value_logic: NaN if claims_t==0 or missing.

definition: Share of month paid from top HCPCS code.
missing_value_logic: NaN if paid_t==0 or no codes.

definition: Share of month paid from top 3 HCPCS codes.
missing_value_logic: NaN if paid_t==0.
notes: If fewer than 3 codes, uses all.

definition: Shannon entropy of HCPCS paid-share distribution.
missing_value_logic: 0 if one code or paid_t==0; NaN only in edge cases.

definition: Herfindahl concentration of paid share by code.
missing_value_logic: NaN if paid_t==0.

definition: Share of month claims from the top HCPCS code by claim count.
missing_value_logic: NaN if claims_t==0 or no codes.

definition: Share of month claims from the top 3 HCPCS codes by claim count.
missing_value_logic: NaN if claims_t==0.
notes: If fewer than 3 codes, uses all. Claim-based analog of top_3_code_paid_share.

definition: Shannon entropy of HCPCS claim-share distribution.
missing_value_logic: 0 if one code or claims_t==0; NaN only in edge cases.

definition: Herfindahl concentration of claim share by HCPCS code.
missing_value_logic: NaN if claims_t==0.

definition: Share of month beneficiary-proxy count from the top HCPCS code. PROXY ONLY — beneficiary counts are summed across HCPCS codes and may double-count beneficiaries appearing under multiple codes.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiary_count absent or beneficiaries_proxy_t==0.
notes: Beneficiary-proxy analog of top_code_paid_share. The _proxy suffix on the feature group is intentional.

definition: Share of month beneficiary-proxy count from the top 3 HCPCS codes. PROXY ONLY — same double-counting caveat as top_code_beneficiary_share_t.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiary_count absent or beneficiaries_proxy_t==0.
notes: If fewer than 3 codes, uses all.

definition: Shannon entropy of HCPCS beneficiary-proxy-share distribution. PROXY ONLY — shares derived from double-counted beneficiary proxy counts.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiary_count absent; 0 if one code.

definition: Herfindahl concentration of beneficiary-proxy share by HCPCS code. PROXY ONLY — same double-counting caveat as other beneficiary-proxy code-mix features.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiary_count absent or beneficiaries_proxy_t==0.

definition: Sum of raw beneficiary_count across all (provider, HCPCS, month) rows. PROXY ONLY — not a true unique-beneficiary count. Summing across HCPCS codes can double-count beneficiaries who appear under multiple codes.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiary_count column is absent from raw data.
notes: Use only as a relative proxy. The _proxy suffix is intentional and must not be removed.

definition: Claims per beneficiary (proxy). PROXY ONLY — denominator is beneficiaries_proxy_t which over-counts shared beneficiaries.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiaries_proxy_t <= 0 or beneficiary_count absent.
notes: Proxy ratio; interpret relative to peers, not as an absolute count.

definition: Paid per beneficiary (proxy). PROXY ONLY — denominator is beneficiaries_proxy_t which over-counts shared beneficiaries.
missing_value_logic: NaN if beneficiaries_proxy_t <= 0 or beneficiary_count absent.
notes: Proxy ratio; interpret relative to peers, not as an absolute dollar figure per patient.

definition: Difference between top-code paid share and top-code claim share. Positive values indicate the top-earning code accounts for a larger portion of dollars than of claims.
missing_value_logic: NaN if either upstream feature is NaN.
notes: Range roughly (-1, 1). Large positive values can indicate up-coding on the dominant code.

definition: Difference between top-code paid share and top-code beneficiary-proxy share. Positive values indicate the top-earning code accounts for a larger portion of dollars than of beneficiaries (proxy). PROXY caveat: beneficiary share is computed from a proxy count.
missing_value_logic: NaN if either upstream feature is NaN.

definition: Difference between paid-based HHI and claim-based HHI. Positive values indicate higher concentration of paid dollars than of claims across HCPCS codes.
missing_value_logic: NaN if either upstream HHI is NaN.
notes: A large positive value suggests that paid dollars are more concentrated than claim counts, which can signal high-value upcoding.

definition: LEIE exclusion label. 1 = provider NPI appears in LEIE; 0 = not excluded.
formula_or_logic: Left join on NPI from provider_labels.csv (build_labels.py).
missing_value_logic: NaN if labels_csv not provided.
notes: Same value repeated across all months for the same NPI.

definition: LEIE exclusion date in YYYYMMDD format. Null for non-excluded providers.
formula_or_logic: Joined from LEIE dataset EXCLDATE column via NPI match.
missing_value_logic: NaN for providers with label=0.
notes: Same value repeated across all months for the same NPI.
